evidence: "If you can get by the unlikely premise, and that the Grounder women can only find low cut t-shirts in the radiated landscape of post apocalyptic Earth, it's a fun, guilty pleasure sci-fi show."
"I enjoyed the show for many years and still enjoy the show even with the changes..."
"The drama seemed overdone from time to time with closeups, quick camera shaking, and music."
"...once the big secret is revealed, I sort of lost interest."
"The last season was wonderful - the tension got more intense with each episode."
"...the story continues at a realistic pace with fine acting and storytelling."
"Production and effects were top notch, and the acting was strong."
"...the animation, which for the most part was very well done..."
"For me, BTAS is the best portrayal of Batman alongside Denny O'Neil's portrayal."
"The music was very cool -- really takes you back to the older films."
"...each contestant appears to really know his survival stuff -- each in, his own way, comes up with solutions to deal with their environment."
"...the death of Apollo is more forceful."
"...the tension got more intense with each episode."

summary: The user enjoys a mix of sci-fi and drama with a strong emphasis on production quality. They appreciate well-crafted narratives but have mixed feelings about pacing and emotional depth, often valuing entertainment and sensory experiences.
